sources.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (answer key for odd-numbered review questions)

meta.notes: Psychology (PSBB) items scraped verbatim from OpenStax Psychology 2e Ch. 15 and Ch. 16 review-questions pages; correct answers from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), odd-numbered review questions only. Ch. 15 Q7 (psbb-x131): the Solutions Guide left this answer blank, so its answer (generalized anxiety disorder) is marked verified-from-source-text (definitionally unambiguous). Live OpenStax pages show CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 plus a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing/non-AI per PRD.

questions.stem: In the harmful dysfunction definition of psychological disorders, dysfunction involves ________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: the inability of a psychological mechanism to perform its function

choices.label: B
choices.text: the breakdown of social order in one's community

choices.label: C
choices.text: communication problems in one's immediate family

questions.explanation: In Wakefield's harmful dysfunction model, dysfunction means an internal psychological mechanism is unable to perform the function it evolved to perform.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax (option A printed as 'a psychological mechanism'). correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q1 = A.

questions.stem: The letters in the abbreviation DSM-5 stand for ________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: Diseases and Statistics Manual of Medicine

choices.label: B
choices.text: Diagnosable Standards Manual of Mental Disorders

choices.label: C
choices.text: Diseases and Symptoms Manual of Mental Disorders

choices.label: D
choices.text: Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders

questions.explanation: DSM-5 stands for the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, fifth edition, the standard U.S. classification of mental disorders.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q3 = D.

questions.stem: The diathesis-stress model presumes that psychopathology results from ________.

choices.label: C
choices.text: chemical imbalances and structural abnormalities in the brain

questions.explanation: The diathesis-stress model holds that psychopathology arises from the interaction of a predisposing vulnerability (diathesis) and stressful or adverse experiences.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q5 = A.

questions.stem: In which of the following anxiety disorders is the person in a continuous state of excessive, pointless worry and apprehension?
questions.explanation: Generalized anxiety disorder is characterized by continuous, excessive, and uncontrollable worry and apprehension that is not tied to a specific object or situation.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. The OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide left the Ch. 15 Q7 answer blank; answer (B = generalized anxiety disorder) independently verified from the definitional OpenStax source text, hence marked verified-from-source-text.

questions.stem: Which of the following best illustrates a compulsion?

choices.label: D
choices.text: falsely believing that a spouse has been cheating

questions.explanation: A compulsion is a repetitive behavior or mental act performed to reduce anxiety. Mentally counting backward is a mental compulsion; the other options describe obsessions or delusions.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q9 = A.

questions.stem: Symptoms of PTSD include all of the following except ________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: intrusive thoughts or memories of a traumatic event

choices.label: B
choices.text: avoidance of things that remind one of a traumatic event

choices.label: D
choices.text: physical complaints that cannot be explained medically

questions.explanation: PTSD symptoms include intrusive memories, avoidance, and heightened arousal (jumpiness). Medically unexplained physical complaints are not a core PTSD symptom.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q11 = D.

questions.stem: Common symptoms of major depressive disorder include all of the following except ________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: periods of extreme elation and euphoria

choices.label: C
choices.text: loss of interest or pleasure in usual activities

questions.explanation: Periods of extreme elation and euphoria are characteristic of mania (bipolar disorder), not major depressive disorder. The other options are common depressive symptoms.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q13 = A.

questions.stem: Clifford falsely believes that the police have planted secret cameras in his home to monitor his every movement. Clifford's belief is an example of ________.
questions.explanation: A fixed, false belief held despite contrary evidence (here, a paranoid belief about surveillance) is a delusion, a positive symptom of psychosis.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q15 = A.

questions.explanation: Dissociative amnesia is an inability to recall important personal information, usually following a stressful or traumatic event, that is too extensive to be ordinary forgetting and is not due to physical injury.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q17 = B.

questions.stem: Which of the following is not a primary characteristic of ADHD?
questions.explanation: Restricted and fixated interests are characteristic of autism spectrum disorder, not ADHD. ADHD is defined by inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q19 = C.

questions.stem: People with borderline personality disorder often ________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: try to be the center of attention

choices.label: D
choices.text: tend to accomplish goals through cruelty

questions.explanation: Borderline personality disorder is characterized by instability in mood, self-image, and relationships, along with marked impulsivity and unpredictability.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q21 = C.

questions.stem: Who of the following does not support the humane and improved treatment of people with mental illness?
questions.explanation: Pinel and Dix were reformers who advocated humane treatment of people with mental illness. Medieval priests, who often attributed mental illness to demonic possession, did not.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 16 Q1 = B.

questions.stem: Joey was convicted of domestic violence. As part of his sentence, the judge has ordered that he attend therapy for anger management. This is considered ________ treatment.
questions.explanation: Court-ordered treatment that a person does not choose for themselves is classified as involuntary treatment.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 16 Q3 = A.

questions.stem: The idea behind ________ is that how you think determines how you feel and act.
questions.explanation: Cognitive therapy is based on the premise that thoughts shape feelings and behavior, so changing maladaptive thinking changes how a person feels and acts.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 16 Q5 = A.

questions.stem: Clay is in a therapy session. The therapist asks him to relax and say whatever comes to his mind at the moment. This therapist is using ________, which is a technique of ________.
questions.explanation: Asking a client to relax and say whatever comes to mind is free association, a core technique of psychoanalysis.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 16 Q7 = D.

choices.label: A
choices.text: The therapist gathers specific information to address the client's immediate needs such as the presenting problem, the client's support system, and insurance status. The therapist informs the client about confidentiality, fees, and what to expect in a therapy session.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The therapist guides what happens in the therapy session and designs a detailed approach to resolving each member's presenting problem.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The therapist meets with a couple to help them see how their individual backgrounds, beliefs, and actions are affecting their relationship.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The therapist examines and discusses with the family the boundaries and structure of the family: For example, who makes the rules, who sleeps in the bed with whom, and how decisions are made.

questions.explanation: An intake is the therapist's first meeting with a client to gather information about presenting problems, support systems, and logistics (confidentiality, fees, expectations).
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 16 Q9 = A.

questions.stem: When an individual has two or more diagnoses, which often includes a substance-related diagnosis and another psychiatric diagnosis, this is known as ________.
questions.explanation: When two or more disorders occur together in the same person, they are described as comorbid disorders (comorbidity).
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 16 Q11 = B.

questions.stem: The sociocultural perspective looks at you, your behaviors, and your symptoms in the context of your ________.
questions.explanation: The sociocultural perspective interprets a person's behavior and symptoms within the context of their culture and background.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 16 Q13 = C.
